page: Amazon Web Services r8gb.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server instance powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features an arm64 CPU architecture with 192 dedicated physical cores and 192 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. With a 1:1 ratio of physical cores to threads, the system avoids hyperthreading, providing dedicated execution resources per vCPU.

The instance is equipped with 1536.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached solutions. To support high-throughput data transfers and network-attached storage, the server provides a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps.

As a bare-metal instance, the r8gb.metal-48xl offers high resource density and direct hardware access, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for large-scale deployments that require massive memory footprints without virtualization overhead. The combination of 192 dedicated Arm64 cores, 1.5 TB of RAM, and 200 Gbps networking makes this server suitable for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale databases, in-memory analytics, and distributed caching systems.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by 192 dedicated AWS Graviton arm64 physical cores running at 2.7 GHz. Designed without hyperthreading, it provides a 1:1 mapping of vCPUs to physical cores for predictable execution. The instance features 1536.0 GB of system memory, establishing an 8.0 GB per core ratio, and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage. This high-density configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for large-scale deployments requiring direct hardware access and massive memory capacity. It is suited for memory-intensive workloads, including large-scale databases, in-memory data grids, and enterprise caching layers.
